control.title: Harden super-dev TDD/implement/build cycle: RED oracle, no-`--lib` parity, scope-aware npm gate, render-layer test parity
control.executiveSummary: Four proven gaps in the Stage 9 TDD→implement→build-gate loop are fixed as a single pure-TS change to pi-super-dev. (1) The TDD "red" phase is currently never verified — `src/stages/implementation.ts:70` calls the `tdd-guide` agent and discards its result, so "TDD" is really "T+D"; we add a deterministic `runRedCheck` oracle and a bounded re-prompt loop. (2) The `buildImplementPrompt` no-`--lib` rule already exists, but `buildTddPrompt` lacks it, so RED checks can miss integration binaries; we mirror it. (3) The cargo `inScopePass`/`outOfScopeErrors` concept is cargo-only, so pre-existing npm/vitest/jest failures block the gate; we generalize it via a shared `touchedFilePaths` git helper. (4) The fgColors-style "real runtime shape vs. mock" bug class passed every gate because unit tests used a mock Theme; we deliver a reusable `withRealTheme` parity helper + whole-render-layer regression + convention doc. All gates degrade safely (never throw); greenfield/in-scope behaviors are unchanged; no new runtime deps; typecheck clean + `npm test` all green. A fully-mechanical "no framework mocks" graph-based gate is explicitly future work.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: `runRedCheck(cwd, testTargets, opts)` exists in `src/build-runner.ts` alongside `runBuildGate`, reusing `detectProjectCommands` (for the runner + `pm`/`language`), `resolveTimeoutMs` (for `spawnSync({timeout})`), the cached cargo-metadata, and the existing scoped-test argv helpers. It runs the project's test command SCOPED to the tdd-guide's reported `testFiles`/targets and returns exactly one status: `"red"` (tests compiled/collected AND ≥1 failed — the desired pre-implement state); `"green"` (tests ran and ALL passed — weak/already-implemented); `"broken"` (compile/collection error — defective test); `"unknown"` (no detectable runner, no test targets, or parse ambiguity — greenfield fallback, MUST NOT block). Per-language invocation: cargo → `cargo test -p <pkg> --test <stem>` per stem (derive stems from testFiles like `resolveIntegrationStems`); vitest/jest → `vitest run <files>` / `npm test -- <files>`; pytest → `pytest <files>`. The function NEVER throws (any error/ambiguity → `unknown` or a conservative status).

`src/stages/implementation.ts` enforces RED inside the existing `attempt` loop: after the tdd-guide agent, it reads the agent's `testFiles` and calls `runRedCheck`, then loops `while status === "green" || status === "broken"` re-prompting the tdd-guide to write genuinely-failing/non-broken tests, capped at `MAX_RED_RETRIES = 2` within the current attempt. On `"red"` OR `"unknown"` it proceeds to the implementer. Every outcome is logged as `Implementation ${phaseId} red-oracle: ${status} (ran: …)`. It NEVER stalls the pipeline: `unknown` always proceeds immediately; on retry-cap exhaustion it proceeds with a loud warning. The implementer prompt is informed that the tests are confirmed-red (so its goal is to green them). The outer `MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3` structure and the `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` commit condition are unchanged.

`buildTddPrompt` (in `src/prompts.ts`) mirrors the no-`--lib` discipline already present in `buildImplementPrompt` via `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE`: the RED phase must run `cargo test -p <pkg>` WITHOUT `--lib` and run any spec-mandated integration target (`cargo test --test <stem>`), so integration tests are covered during the RED check, not just lib unit tests. The existing `rustDiscipline(s)` helper is reused (Rust-only, gated on `s.language === "rust"`); no change to the already-correct `buildImplementPrompt` text is required beyond confirming both builders share the same source string.

`runBuildGate` generalizes in-scope/out-of-scope classification to npm/vitest/jest projects. After a failed non-cargo test run it parses failing TEST FILE paths from runner output (vitest `❯ path/to/file.test.ts`; jest `FAIL src/file.test.ts`), computes the touched-file set via a NEW shared `touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef)` helper that extracts the existing git `diff --merge-base` + `ls-files --others` union currently embedded in `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (so NO new git spawn beyond what touched-file detection already does), classifies each failing test file as OUT-OF-SCOPE if NOT in the touched set, and populates `outOfScopeErrors`/`inScopePass` exactly like the cargo path. NEVER throws; on any parse ambiguity → treat as IN-SCOPE (conservative, grants no false green). The cargo `inScopePass` behavior is unchanged byte-for-byte.

`tests/helpers/real-theme.ts` exports `withRealTheme<T>(fn: (theme: Theme) => T): T` that calls `initTheme()` (idempotent) and passes the real class-based proxy `theme` to `fn`. It NEVER destructures Theme methods (guards `this.fgColors`); callers use method-style `theme.fg(...)` or a wrapper. `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` runs `themeLine`, `commandBackground`, `buildResultComponent`, `packDashboardLines`, and `createDashboardWidgetFactory`'s output through `withRealTheme` and asserts no-throw + ANSI output, generalizing `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` into a shared regression for the WHOLE render layer. `docs/testing-parity.md` documents the convention: any module wrapping a framework type behind a structural interface MUST have ≥1 parity test via `withRealTheme`/`initTheme`; mock-only coverage of a class-based dependency is a known false-green.

Backward compatibility preserved: greenfield repos (no runner/no tests) → `runRedCheck` returns `unknown` → proceeds exactly as today (no new spawn aborts); repos with no test failures are unchanged; cargo `inScopePass` is unchanged; npm repos with only in-scope failures still block correctly; repos whose suite has pre-existing failures in untouched files now pass via `inScopePass`. `npm run typecheck` is strict-clean. `npm test` is ALL green — the existing large build-runner suite (scoped-args, touched-crates, autoscope, inscope-classification, resolver-validation, nonregression, package-wiring) plus the new red-oracle + parity + npm-inscope suites. Any helper-signature change that affects existing tests is updated IN THE SAME change.

No regression to existing behaviors: dashboard widget, themed stream, scope-aware cargo gate, mid-run input injection, and Markdown §3 artifacts. No change to the subprocess/session backend or control-flow engine (`nodes.ts`/`workflow.ts`/`pipeline.ts`); no change to `MAX_ATTEMPTS` (3) or the Stage 10 review / Stage 11 integration structure; no new runtime dependencies.

nonFunctional: Performance: `runRedCheck` spawns one scoped test command per attempt using the same cached timeout envelope (`resolveTimeoutMs`) as `runBuildGate`; the npm in-scope path adds NO new git process beyond what `detectTouchedCargoPackages` already spawns (it reuses the extracted `touchedFilePaths` helper). RED re-prompts are capped at `MAX_RED_RETRIES = 2` so the worst-case cost per phase is `2 tdd-guide + 2 red-check + 1 implementer + build-gate`, bounded and never infinite.

Reliability / fail-safe: EVERY new gate/oracle/git helper degrades instead of throwing — red oracle → `unknown` → proceed; in-scope parse ambiguity → conservative IN-SCOPE (never a false green); git/spawn errors → empty touched set → conservative in-scope. No path can stall or abort the pipeline except the existing genuine in-scope gate failure after `MAX_ATTEMPTS`.

Correctness: the fix targets the specific failure class (fgColors) — a class-based framework type whose methods read `this.fgColors` and crash only on a detached call. Mocks hide this; the real Theme proxy via `withRealTheme` exposes it deterministically.

Type safety: TypeScript strict-clean (`npm run typecheck`); no `any` leaks across the new boundaries; new helpers reuse existing typed interfaces (`ProjectCommands`, `BuildGateResult`, `GateOptions`).

Maintainability: the convention is codified in `docs/testing-parity.md` so future render-layer modules have a checklist, and the shared `touchedFilePaths` helper eliminates the duplicated git-union logic.
Scope/blast-radius: pure-TS change inside pi-super-dev only; zero changes to the control-flow engine, session backend, or external service contracts.
openQuestions: Should `runRedCheck`'s `red` classification require a MINIMUM number of failing tests (e.g. ≥1 is the spec default), or should it additionally warn when the tdd-guide reported `testsWritten > 0` but `testFiles` resolved to zero runnable targets (a likely defective guide output)? Recommended: warn-and-treat-as-`broken` to force a re-prompt, since the guide's own contract says it wrote tests.

AC-04 touches the npm path but pytest output formatting is runner/version-dependent. Recommended scope: explicitly support vitest + jest file-path markers now (the two `pm`-driven node runners this repo uses), and degrade to conservative IN-SCOPE for any unrecognized pytest output rather than over-fitting — confirm pytest is in-scope or out-of-scope for THIS repo before shipping.

For `runRedCheck` on cargo: the spec's `gate.integration` stems are validated at build-gate time via `resolveIntegrationStems`. Should the RED oracle re-validate the same stems, or trust the tdd-guide's raw `testFiles`? Recommended: trust the guide's `testFiles` for the RED check (it is testing freshly-written tests, which may not yet be valid stems), and keep validation only at the build gate — avoids a false `broken` from over-eager validation.

The `MAX_RED_RETRIES = 2` constant: confirm it should be a module-level const in `src/stages/implementation.ts` (mirroring `MAX_ATTEMPTS`) and not configurable via spec/gate. Recommended: module-level const, no new config surface, matching the task's explicit `MAX_RED_RETRIES = 2` directive.

control.title: Hardening the super-dev TDD/build cycle: RED oracle, no-`--lib` parity, scope-aware npm gate, render-layer test parity
control.summary: All four gaps map onto well-established practices and onto patterns this repo already implements for cargo — so the work is principled extension, not invention. (1) The proposed runRedCheck status taxonomy (red/green/broken/unknown) is the classic Beck TDD RED→GREEN→REFACTOR cycle (Kent Beck, *Test-Driven Development: By Example*, 2002 [SRC-01]) with two recognized refinements: "broken" = the well-known "false red" (a compile/collection error, not a logic failure — pytest emits `ERROR` vs `FAILED`, vitest emits collection errors vs failures [SRC-02]), and "unknown" = the defensive-programming escape hatch that keeps greenfield repos from stalling. (2) The render-layer parity bug (fgColors crash) is a textbook "don't mock types you don't own" / Classical-vs-Mockist case (Martin Fowler, "Mocks Aren't Stubs," 2007 [SRC-03]; Freeman & Pryce, *Growing OO Software*, 2009 [SRC-04]): a structural-interface mock survived while the real class-based Theme threw on a detached `this.fgColors` read; the durable fix is testing through the REAL object (`withRealTheme`), which is the established "integration unit test / parity test" mitigation. (3) Generalizing in-scope/out-of-scope test classification from cargo to npm/vitest/jest mirrors standard CI changed-file scoping (nx `affected`, turbo `--filter`, Bazel [SRC-05]) and is already half-built here: `classifyOutOfScopeErrors` (build-runner.ts:690), `inScopePass = pass || (errors.length>0 && outOfScopeErrors.length===errors.length)`, and the git `diff --merge-base` + `ls-files --others` union inside `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (build-runner.ts:485–545) — `touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef)` is a pure extraction of that union, reusing the SAME spawns so no new git process is introduced. (4) The no-`--lib` discipline is already implemented in `rustDiscipline`/`RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE` (prompts.ts:99–106) and shared by buildImplementPrompt+buildQaPrompt; Gap 3 is only threading it into buildTddPrompt. NOTE: live web sources were unreachable in this sandbox (no network); claims rest on canonical references and the repo's proven, regression-guarded patterns rather than freshly fetched docs.

options.name: 4-state RED taxonomy (red/green/broken/unknown) over a 2-state pass/fail
options.tradeoffs: Distinguishing 'broken' (defective test, compile/collection error) from 'red' (genuine assertion failure) prevents the implementer from chasing a syntax error as if it were a failing test, and keeps the re-prompt loop meaningful (broken→rewrite test; green→rewrite to actually fail). 'unknown' is the critical never-stall escape hatch for greenfield/ambiguous repos. Cost: per-runner parse heuristics (pytest ERROR vs FAILED; vitest collection-error phrasing; jest compile errors) — stable but must be pinned in mocked-spawnSync tests so a runner output change can't silently widen 'broken' into 'red'. The 4 states map directly onto signals the runners already emit, so the extra state is cheap.

options.name: MAX_RED_RETRIES=2 with proceed-on-exhaustion warning (vs hard-fail vs unbounded)
options.tradeoffs: Bounded-with-warning preserves the never-stall invariant AND bounds worst-case cost per phase (≤2 tdd-guide + 2 red-check + 1 impl + build-gate), which matters given past 'empty-control' loop blowups in this pipeline (learned.md). Hard-fail would block greenfield repos that cannot easily produce a true red; unbounded risks infinite loops — a documented prior failure class here. Tradeoff: a phase that genuinely can't write a failing test proceeds 'red-unconfirmed', so the gate (runBuildGate) remains the final hard oracle — the RED loop is an early guard, not the trust boundary.

options.name: Extract shared touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef) rather than duplicate git logic per runner
options.tradeoffs: Pure extraction of the exact `diff --merge-base --name-only` + `ls-files --others --exclude-standard` union already inside detectTouchedCargoPackages (build-runner.ts:485–545) — reuses the SAME two spawns so the 'no new git spawn' invariant holds. Keeps the cargo and npm classification paths provably consistent (same touched set → same notion of in-scope). Tradeoff: touches a large, regression-guarded file (the existing build-runner suite is broad), so the refactor MUST keep the cargo path byte-identical — the current CRATE_SEGMENT_RE filtering stays inside detectTouchedCargoPackages, with only the raw-path union lifted into the shared helper.

options.name: withRealTheme callback helper + conservative-ambiguity in-scope classification (vs a mechanical no-mocks gate vs per-test hand-rolled ClassTheme)
options.tradeoffs: withRealTheme(initTheme→real proxy, forbid destructuring) is the pragmatic durable middle: it catches the detached-`this.fgColors` class that mocks miss, costs one line per test, and generalizes the existing stream-theme-class-theme test across the whole render layer (themeLine, commandBackground, buildResultComponent, packDashboardLines, createDashboardWidgetFactory). A fully mechanical 'no mocks of framework types' graph-based gate is infeasible without a type-dependency graph — correctly deferred as future work. Per-test hand-rolled ClassTheme (current) doesn't scale. Conservative npm ambiguity→in-scope (never grants a false green) is the correct fail-safe direction, mirroring the existing cargo classifyOutOfScopeErrors 'mixed/ambiguous→kept in errors' rule.

openIssues: Live web sources were unreachable in this sandbox (no network via curl). Claims rest on canonical references — Beck 2002 (TDD: By Example) [SRC-01], pytest ERROR-vs-FAILED semantics [SRC-02], Fowler 'Mocks Aren't Stubs' 2007 [SRC-03], Freeman & Pryce 2009 [SRC-04], changed-file CI scoping (nx/turbo/Bazel) [SRC-05] — and on this repo's own regression-guarded patterns, not freshly fetched docs. Confirm against current docs before relying on exact runner output strings.

broken-vs-red classification depends on per-runner output heuristics (pytest 'ERROR' vs 'FAILED'; vitest collection-error phrasing; jest compile/transform errors). These strings are stable but should be PINNED in the mocked-spawnSync unit tests so a runner upgrade can't silently widen 'broken' into 'red' (which would let a defective test masquerade as a real red).

Path-normalization mismatch risk for npm in-scope: touched paths from `git diff --merge-base` are repo-rooted; failing-test paths from vitest ('❯ path/to/file.test.ts') and jest ('FAIL src/file.test.ts') may be cwd- or workspace-relative. touchedFilePaths must normalize (resolve/relative) before set-membership, or every failing file silently reads as in-scope (safe-but-useless — grants no false green, but the feature becomes inert). Confirm the normalize step is exercised by the npm-inscope test.

MAX_RED_RETRIES interacts with MAX_ATTEMPTS: the 2 retries live INSIDE one attempt; an exhausted-then-proceeded phase still advances normally to the implementer and gate. The spec asserts the cost bound is non-multiplicative (worst case 2+2+1+gate per attempt, attempts capped at 3), but a test pinning the worst-case spawn count per phase would lock the invariant against a future edit that nests the loops wrong.

runRedCheck reuses resolveTimeoutMs for the spawnSync envelope, but a scoped per-stem `cargo test --test <stem>` loop across many stems could exceed a single timeout window. Confirm whether the timeout wraps the whole loop or per-stem invocation, and that a mid-loop timeout degrades to 'unknown' (not a thrown SyncError) per AC-01/SCENARIO-005.

control.title: Hardening the super-dev TDD/implement/build cycle: deterministic RED oracle, npm in-scope gating, and test-parity for class-based dependencies
control.summary: All five prior open issues are now resolved against live sources and the repo's own code. Network IS reachable this session (HTTP 200), so runner output strings are confirmed current: pytest reports ERROR (collection/setup failure ⇒ broken), FAILED (test ran, assertion failed ⇒ red), PASSED (green) [SRC-03, docs.pytest.org/en/stable/how-to/output.html]; jest prints a per-file `FAIL src/file.test.ts` prefix [SRC-02, jestjs.io/docs/cli]; vitest prints `❯ path/to/file.test.ts` per failing file plus `fail`/`failed` counts [SRC-01, vitest.dev/guide/cli.html]. The repo already encodes the correct primitives: `resolveTimeoutMs` is applied per-`spawnSync` (build-runner.ts:1034), the `exec` closure try/catches ANY thrown spawn and degrades to an error entry (never escapes the gate), and `detectTouchedCargoPackages` already computes the `git diff --merge-base` + `ls-files --others` touched-file union — so a shared `touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef)` is a pure extraction, not new spawning [SRC-04]. The four gaps map to: (1) a `runRedCheck` that reuses `detectProjectCommands`+`resolveTimeoutMs`+`resolveIntegrationStems`+the cached cargo metadata, loops per-stem with a per-invocation timeout, and never throws; (2) an implementation.ts RED-enforcement loop capped at MAX_RED_RETRIES=2 inside the existing MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 attempt; (3) mirroring the existing `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE` constant into `buildTddPrompt` via the already-correct `rustDiscipline(s)` helper (confirmed buildTddPrompt does NOT yet call it [SRC-04]); (4) generalizing cargo `inScopePass`/`outOfScopeErrors` to npm/vitest/jest via path-normalized touched-set membership. The canonical TDD red-before-green discipline [SRC-05, Beck 2002] makes the RED oracle the load-bearing fix. The options below pin each previously-ambiguous invariant with a targeted test, so a future edit cannot silently widen broken→red, read a workspace-relative path as in-scope, nest the retry loop into a cost blow-up, or let a mid-stem timeout throw.

options.name: Pin RED-classifier strings as named regex constants + full-blob status tests (resolves broken-vs-red drift, open issue #2)
options.tradeoffs: Define classifier regexes (e.g. PYTEST_ERROR=/\bERROR\b/, PYTEST_FAILED=/\bFAILED\b/, PYTEST_PASSED=/passed|PASSED/; jest /^FAIL /m; vitest failed/❯ + collection-error /failed to load|cannot find module/i) as module-level named constants in build-runner.ts, and assert the FULL mocked-spawnSync output blob → expected status in the red-oracle unit test. PRO: a runner upgrade that changes phrasing breaks the pinning test loudly instead of silently widening broken→red (a defective test masquerading as a real red); classification order (broken-regex checked BEFORE failed-regex) is also pinned. CON: a few extra test fixtures; strings must be touched when a runner changes its reporter — but that breakage is exactly the desired signal. Status precedence: broken (compile/collection error) → red (≥1 FAILED) → green (all passed) → unknown (parse ambiguity/no targets/no runner), matching AC-01.

options.name: Per-stem spawnSync with per-invocation timeout + SIGTERM→unknown degradation (resolves multi-stem timeout, open issue #5)
options.tradeoffs: runRedCheck mirrors runBuildGate's exec exactly: a for loop over stems (cargo: cargo test -p <pkg> --test <stem>; vitest/jest: single vitest run <files>/npm test -- <files>; pytest: pytest <files>), each spawnSync(argv, {cwd, timeout: timeoutMs}) wrapped in try/catch. A timeout sets r.signal==='SIGTERM'/r.status===null → classify unknown (proceed), NEVER throw and NEVER emit a SyncError. PRO: matches the existing proven pattern byte-for-byte; each scoped test is cheap (single stem) so a per-invocation envelope is correct, not the whole loop; mid-loop timeout degrades cleanly per AC-01/SCENARIO-005. CON: N stems × timeout budget worst-case, but stems are derived from the tdd-guide's own reported testFiles (small N), and runRedCheck is advisory (never blocks on unknown). The vitest/jest/pytest paths run ONCE (files passed as args), so only cargo loops — and cargo scoped test binaries are individually fast.

options.name: Path-normalize touched-set membership via path.resolve/relative before set comparison (resolves npm in-scope normalization, open issue #3)
options.tradeoffs:
Extract touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef): string[] from the existing git union in detectTouchedCargoPackages (returns repo-rooted paths), then build the membership set as new Set(touched.map(p => path.resolve(cwd, p))). Parse failing-test paths from runner output (vitest ❯

; jest FAIL

), normalize each via path.resolve(cwd, raw) (handles cwd-/workspace-relative) and test membership against the resolved set. A failing file NOT in the set ⇒ out-of-scope. PRO: resolve collapses all of ./, ../, cwd-relative, and absolute-under-cwd to one canonical form, so a workspace-relative vitest path matches the repo-rooted git path; the npm-inscope test exercises a cwd-relative failing path to pin this. CON: on parse ambiguity (unparseable path line) → classify IN-SCOPE (conservative: never grants a false green, per AC-04). NEVER throws: wrap parsing in try/catch, default-to-in-scope on failure. Cargo inScopePass stays byte-identical.

options.name: Assert ≤(MAX_RED_RETRIES+1) runRedCheck calls/attempt + retry-exhaustion proceeds (resolves cost-bound non-multiplicativity, open issue #4)
options.tradeoffs: MAX_RED_RETRIES=2 lives INSIDE one attempt; worst case per attempt = 1 initial tdd-guide + runRedCheck, then up to 2 retry rounds (re-prompt tdd-guide + runRedCheck) = 3 runRedCheck spawns max/attempt, plus 1 implementer + 1 gate. Across MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 that is ≤9 advisory red-oracle spawns total (non-multiplicative with the gate). Pin a unit test that stubs runRedCheck to always return 'green' and asserts it is called exactly MAX_RED_RETRIES+1 (=3) times per attempt, AND that after exhaustion the phase proceeds to the implementer + gate (loud warning) rather than looping the outer attempt. PRO: a future edit that accidentally nests the retry loop inside the attempt loop (multiplying cost) breaks this test. CON: a test counter coupling to the constant; acceptable since the constant is load-bearing and documented. Implementation.ts treats gate.pass || gate.inScopePass as commit-green — unchanged. unknown always proceeds immediately (0 retries), red proceeds immediately (0 retries); only green/broken retry.

options.name: Mirror RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE into buildTddPrompt via the existing rustDiscipline(s) helper (Gap 3)
options.tradeoffs: The RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE constant and rustDiscipline(s) helper already exist and are shared by buildImplementPrompt + buildQaPrompt; buildTddPrompt confirmed to NOT yet use them [SRC-04]. Fix is a one-line append of rustDiscipline(s) to buildTddPrompt's Instructions array, gated on s.language==='rust' (no change to non-Rust stacks). PRO: zero new constants, single source of truth for the no-`--lib` discipline; RED checks then run full cargo test -p <pkg> (covering tests/ integration binaries) not just lib unit tests. CON: none — it is the documented AC-03 intent; buildImplementPrompt text needs no change beyond confirming both share the same constant.

options.name: withRealTheme parity helper + whole-render-layer parity test (Gap 2; honest about graph-gate limits)
options.tradeoffs: tests/helpers/real-theme.ts exports withRealTheme<T>(fn) that calls initTheme() (idempotent) and passes the real class-based proxy theme; callers use method-style theme.fg() (NEVER destructure — the fgColors-class guard). tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts runs themeLine, commandBackground, buildResultComponent, packDashboardLines, createDashboardWidgetFactory through it asserting no-throw + ANSI. PRO: catches the fgColors detached-method class of bug that plain-object mocks miss (a known false-green), and generalizes tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts to the whole render layer. CON: a fully-mechanical 'no framework mocks' graph-based gate is NOT feasible without a type-dependency graph — acknowledge as future work in docs/testing-parity.md; the parity convention is the durable, proportionate fix (≥1 parity test per module wrapping a framework type behind a structural interface).

result.text:
I now have complete evidence for all four gaps. Let me compose the root-cause analysis.

**Confirmed findings:**

- **Gap 1**: `implementation.ts:49` — `await ctx.agent({...tdd-guide...})` discards the return value; `runRedCheck`/`MAX_RED_RETRIES` do not exist. The `buildTddPrompt` asks for `{testsWritten, testFiles, allFailing}` but nothing consumes it. "TDD" = "T+D".
- **Gap 3**: `rustDiscipline(s)` is wired into `buildImplementPrompt` and `buildQaPrompt` but **NOT** `buildTddPrompt` (it only emits `langInstructions`).
- **Gap 4**: `runBuildGate` sets `classScope` only when `cmds0.language === "rust" && testPackages.length > 0`; the npm path leaves `testPackages=[]` → `classifyOutOfScopeErrors(errors, [])` returns ALL errors in-scope (the empty-scope conservative branch). No vitest `❯`/jest `FAIL` parsing, no shared `touchedFilePaths`.
- **Gap 2**: `fgColors` regression guarded by a single file (`stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts`) covering `themeLine` only; no `tests/helpers/real-theme.ts`, no whole-render-layer parity test.

control.title: Root-Cause Analysis: Four Gaps in the super-dev TDD/Build Cycle (red-oracle, --lib discipline, npm scope, framework-type parity)
control.summary: Stage 9's skeleton is correct — a deterministic per-phase `tdd-guide → implementer → runBuildGate` loop (hard build/test oracle) committing on `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` — but four independent gaps let real bugs slip to runtime this session. The unifying root cause across all four is that super-dev **trusts LLM self-report or unscoped/default classification at exactly the weak seams**: (1) the TDD "red" phase is never verified mechanically (the tdd-guide's `{testsWritten, testFiles, allFailing}` return is awaited and discarded at `src/stages/implementation.ts:49`, so already-passing or absent tests pass as "TDD"); (2) the TDD prompt never forbids `--lib`-only self-verification, so the RED check only sees cargo lib unit tests and silently skips the `tests/` integration binaries; (3) out-of-scope failure classification (the cargo `inScopePass` feature that lets pre-existing breakage in untouched crates not abort in-scope work) is gated to Rust only — the npm/vitest/jest path computes `classScope = []` and `classifyOutOfScopeErrors(errors, [])` conservatively marks EVERY error in-scope, so any pre-existing failing test file the branch never touched hard-blocks the gate; (4) the `fgColors` crash class (detaching a method from the class-based pi `Theme` so `fg()` reads an undefined `this.fgColors`) is guarded by exactly ONE regression test (`tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts`) over `themeLine` only — there is no shared real-object parity helper and no test over the rest of the render layer (`commandBackground`, `buildResultComponent`, `packDashboardLines`, `createDashboardWidgetFactory`), so a mock-only coverage of a class-based dependency is a known false-green everywhere else. All four were confirmed by reading the source. None requires any change to the control-flow engine (`nodes.ts`/`workflow.ts`/`pipeline.ts`) or to `MAX_ATTEMPTS`; each is a localized TS addition/orchestration in `src/build-runner.ts`, `src/stages/implementation.ts`, `src/prompts.ts`, plus one test helper + one parity test + one doc. The fixes are backward-compatible and never-throw: greenfield repos (no runner/no tests) and untouched-passing suites behave byte-identically today; every new oracle/helper degrades to a non-blocking status on any ambiguity rather than aborting the pipeline.
hypotheses: H1 (Gap 1, p≈0.95) — the TDD 'red' phase is never verified mechanically. Prediction: in `src/stages/implementation.ts` the `tdd-guide` agent call awaits but discards its control value, and no `runRedCheck` symbol exists in `src/`. CONFIRMED: `implementation.ts:49` does `await ctx.agent({ id: ..., agent: "tdd-guide", prompt: buildTddPrompt(...) })` with no `const tdd = ...`, and `buildTddPrompt` (src/prompts.ts) explicitly returns `{testsWritten, testFiles, allFailing}` that nothing consumes. rg `runRedCheck|MAX_RED_RETRIES` in src/ → no matches. So tests that already pass, or are never written, sail through as 'TDD'. Falsifiable: a phase whose tdd-guide returns `allFailing:false` still proceeds straight to the implementer today.

H1.a (Gap 1 fallback, p≈0.85) — when a red oracle is added it MUST not stall greenfield pipelines or loop forever. Prediction: the correct design returns a 4th status `unknown` (no runner / no targets / parse ambiguity) that proceeds, and caps re-prompt retries at a small constant. This matches `runBuildGate`'s existing greenfield non-fatal pattern (`ran:[], pass:true`) and the cargo `inScopePass` never-throw contract.

H2 (Gap 3, p≈0.9) — the no-`--lib` self-verify discipline is wired into the implementer/QA prompts but NOT the TDD prompt. Prediction: `rustDiscipline(s)` (or `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE`) appears in `buildImplementPrompt` and `buildQaPrompt` but is absent from `buildTddPrompt`'s instruction list. CONFIRMED: src/prompts.ts defines `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE` + `rustDiscipline(s)` and spreads `...(rust ? [rust] : [])` only in `buildImplementPrompt` and `buildQaPrompt`; `buildTddPrompt` emits only `langInstructions`. So a tdd-guide that self-verifies with `cargo test -p <pkg> --lib` reports RED based solely on lib unit tests and the `tests/` integration binaries are never exercised before the gate.

H3 (Gap 4, p≈0.9) — out-of-scope classification is Rust-only by construction, so npm pre-existing failures always block. Prediction: in `runBuildGate`, the classification scope `classScope` is non-empty ONLY on the `cmds0.language === "rust" && testPackages.length > 0` branch; on the npm/vitest/jest branch `testPackages` is `[]`, so `classifyOutOfScopeErrors(errors, [])` hits its empty-scope branch and returns ALL errors as in-scope. CONFIRMED: `runBuildGate` computes `testPackages=[]` for non-rust repos (only the rust branch assigns touched/auto packages), then `const classScope = cmds0.language === "rust" && testPackages.length > 0 ? classificationScope(...) : testPackages` → `[]`; `classifyOutOfScopeErrors` returns `outOfScopeErrors:[]` whenever `scope.size === 0`; and `inScopePass = pass || (errors.length>0 && outOfScopeErrors.length===errors.length)` is therefore false whenever any npm test fails, regardless of which file. There is also no failing-test-FILE parsing (vitest `❯ path/to/file.test.ts` / jest `FAIL src/file.test.ts`) and no shared `touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef)` helper — the touched-file union (`git diff --merge-base` + `git ls-files --others`) is currently inlined inside `detectTouchedCargoPackages` and applied only to `crates/<seg>/` lines.

H4 (Gap 2, p≈0.8) — the fgColors crash class is guarded by a single regression test over one render function, with no real-object parity coverage for the rest of the render layer. Prediction: only `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` exists for this bug class, it exercises `themeLine` (src/render/stream-theme.ts:139) only, and there is no `tests/helpers/real-theme.ts` and no test running `commandBackground` (stream-theme.ts:206), `buildResultComponent` (render/dashboard.ts:360), `packDashboardLines` (dashboard.ts:208), or `createDashboardWidgetFactory` (dashboard.ts:295) against the real class-based Theme. CONFIRMED via rg: the only `initTheme`+fgColors regression is stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts; `withRealTheme` does not exist anywhere. So any render path that a future refactor attaches a plain-object mock Theme to can crash at runtime on a detached `theme.fg(...)` while unit tests stay green.

H5 (alternate, p≈0.05) — one of the four failures is actually a backend/control-flow regression (signal abort, budget exhaustion, or a stage emitting empty `control:{}`). RULED OUT: `runBuildGate` already try/catches every spawn (SCENARIO-034/AC-02) and degrades non-fatally; `MAX_ATTEMPTS=3` and the Stage 10 review / Stage 11 integration structure are explicitly out of scope and untouched. The four gaps are localized prompt/orchestration/classification defects, not pipeline-engine failures.
control.rootCause:
Four INDEPENDENT defects, each a localized trust-seam in `src/` (no control-flow-engine change required):

(1) GAP 1 — RED phase never verified. `src/stages/implementation.ts:49` runs `await ctx.agent({ id: `pipeline.implementation.${phaseId}.tdd.a${attempt}`, agent: \"tdd-guide\", prompt: buildTddPrompt(...) })` and DISCARDS the result; `buildTddPrompt` (src/prompts.ts) returns `{testsWritten, testFiles, allFailing}` that is never read, and `runRedCheck`/`MAX_RED_RETRIES` do not exist (rg → 0 matches in src/). The loop trusts the tdd-guide's self-report that tests were written and are failing. FIX: add `runRedCheck(cwd, testTargets, opts)` to `src/build-runner.ts` (reuse `detectProjectCommands` for runner/language, `resolveTimeoutMs` for the `spawnSync({timeout})` envelope, cached cargo metadata, and the existing scoped-argv + `resolveIntegrationStems`-style stem derivation) returning exactly one of `red`/`green`/`broken`/`unknown`, NEVER throwing (`unknown` = no runner/no targets/parse ambiguity → proceed); wire `implementation.ts` to read the tdd-guide's `testFiles`, call `runRedCheck`, and `while status === green || broken` re-prompt the tdd-guide capped at `MAX_RED_RETRIES = 2`, logging `Implementation ${phaseId} red-oracle: ${status} (ran: …)`, proceeding on `red`/`unknown` (unknown + retry-cap proceed with a loud warning), and telling the implementer prompt the tests are confirmed-red.

(2) GAP 3 — `--lib` discipline missing from the RED prompt. `rustDiscipline(s)` (src/prompts.ts) spreads `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE` into `buildImplementPrompt` and `buildQaPrompt` but NOT `buildTddPrompt` (it emits only `langInstructions`). FIX: append `rustDiscipline(s)` to `buildTddPrompt` too so the RED check runs `cargo test -p <pkg>` WITHOUT `--lib` plus any spec-mandated `cargo test --test <stem>`.

(3) GAP 4 — npm out-of-scope classification disabled by construction. In `runBuildGate` (src/build-runner.ts) `testPackages` is non-empty ONLY on the rust branch; npm/vitest/jest leaves it `[]`, so `classScope` (line `cmds0.language === \"rust\" && testPackages.length > 0 ? classificationScope(...) : testPackages`) is `[]`, and `classifyOutOfScopeErrors(errors, [])` returns ALL errors in-scope (its empty-scope conservative branch). FIX: extract the git union now inlined in `detectTouchedCargoPackages` into a shared `touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef)` (no NEW git spawn beyond what touched-file detection already does), parse failing TEST FILE paths from runner output (vitest `❯ path/to/file.test.ts`; jest `FAIL src/file.test.ts`) when a non-cargo test run failed, classify a failing file OUT-OF-SCOPE iff NOT in the touched set, and populate `outOfScopeErrors`/`inScopePass` exactly like the cargo path; on any parse ambiguity treat as IN-SCOPE (conservative, no false green). Cargo behavior stays byte-identical.

(4) GAP 2 — framework-type parity covered for one function only. Only `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` exercises the real class-based pi `Theme` (`fg()` reads `this.fgColors`; a detached method call throws \"reading 'fgColors'\"), and only over `themeLine` (src/render/stream-theme.ts:139). No `tests/helpers/real-theme.ts` `withRealTheme<T>(fn)`, no whole-render-layer parity test. FIX: add `tests/helpers/real-theme.ts` exporting `withRealTheme<T>(fn: (theme: Theme)=>T): T` (calls idempotent `initTheme()`, passes the real proxy, NEVER destructures methods), a `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` running `themeLine`, `commandBackground` (stream-theme.ts:206), `buildResultComponent` (render/dashboard.ts:360), `packDashboardLines` (dashboard.ts:208), and `createDashboardWidgetFactory` (dashboard.ts:295) through it asserting no-throw + ANSI output, and `docs/testing-parity.md` documenting: any module wrapping a framework type behind a structural interface MUST have ≥1 parity test via `withRealTheme`/`initTheme`; mock-only coverage of a class-based dependency is a known false-green. (A fully-mechanical graph-based 'no framework mocks' gate is NOT feasible without a type-dependency graph and is explicitly acknowledged as future work.)

All four are backward-compatible and never-throw; `npm run typecheck` strict-clean and `npm test` green (existing build-runner suite + new red-oracle, parity, and npm-inscope suites) are the acceptance signal.
reproductionSteps: GAP 1 (red-oracle never runs): `rg -n 'runRedCheck|MAX_RED_RETRIES' src` → 0 matches. Then open `src/stages/implementation.ts` and confirm line ~49 `await ctx.agent({ id: ...`.tdd.a${attempt}., agent: "tdd-guide", prompt: buildTddPrompt(...) })` has no left-hand assignment — the tdd-guide's `testFiles`/`allFailing` are discarded. Reproduce the false-RED behavior: in a scratch TS repo, hand the tdd-guide a phase whose spec test ALREADY passes (or write no test); the loop proceeds straight to the implementer with no RED assertion (status today is effectively 'always proceed'). After fix: `runRedCheck` returns `green`/`broken` and the loop re-prompts ≤2× before a loud proceed.

GAP 3 (--lib discipline missing in TDD prompt): open `src/prompts.ts`, diff `buildTddPrompt` vs `buildImplementPrompt`/`buildQaPrompt` — the latter two spread `...(rust ? [rust] : [])` from `rustDiscipline(s)`; `buildTddPrompt` does not. Reproduce: on a rust repo with a failing `tests/<stem>.rs` integration binary but a passing lib, the current tdd-guide self-verifying with `cargo test -p <pkg> --lib` would report RED=false-y / pass and the integration target would be invisible until the implementer gate.

GAP 4 (npm out-of-scope always blocks): `node -e "const{classifyOutOfScopeErrors}=require('./dist/build-runner.js'); console.log(classifyOutOfScopeErrors(['FAIL tests/legacy.test.ts'], []))"` → `{inScopeErrors:['FAIL tests/legacy.test.ts'], outOfScopeErrors:[]}` (empty scope → all in-scope). End-to-end: in a vitest repo, leave a PRE-EXISTING failing test in an UNTOUCHED file, run `runBuildGate(cwd, {gate:...})`; `inScopePass` is `false` even though the branch touched nothing in that file. After fix: parse the vitest `❯ path/to/file.test.ts` / jest `FAIL src/file.test.ts` line, intersect with `touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef)` (the `git diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` ∪ `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` union), and a file NOT in the touched set populates `outOfScopeErrors` → `inScopePass` true. Cargo path unchanged: re-run the existing `build-runner-inscope-classification.test.ts` / `build-runner-touched-crates.test.ts` to confirm byte-identical behavior.

GAP 2 (render-layer parity hole): `rg -n 'withRealTheme|initTheme' tests/` → `initTheme` only in `stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` and `dashboard-result.test.ts`; `withRealTheme` → 0. Reproduce the crash class: synthetically detach — `const { fg } = theme; fg('accent','x')` throws "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'fgColors')" against the real Theme, while a plain-object mock `({fg:(c,v)=>v})` does NOT, so any render path unit-tested with such a mock is a false-green. Confirm coverage hole: `rg -n 'commandBackground|buildResultComponent|packDashboardLines|createDashboardWidgetFactory' tests/` shows they are exercised only with mock/structural themes, never via `initTheme`. After fix: `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` runs all five through `withRealTheme` and asserts no-throw + ANSI output.

REGRESSION GUARD (whole-suite): `npm run typecheck` strict-clean and `npm test` ALL green — the existing build-runner suite (scoped-args, touched-crates, autoscope, inscope-classification, resolver-validation, nonregression, package-wiring, timeout, backward-compat-regression, docs) plus the new red-oracle, real-theme-parity, and npm-inscope suites; plus `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` still green (no Theme-method destructuring introduced anywhere).

control.title: Codebase Assessment — Hardening the super-dev TDD/Build Cycle (RED oracle, npm in-scope gate, test-parity)
control.summary: pi-super-dev is a self-contained, composable control-flow pipeline (13 stages, ~21 spawned pi subagents) packaged as a pi extension/library. The hardening work is concentrated in three modules: `src/build-runner.ts` (the deterministic build/test/typecheck "hard oracle", 1111 lines), `src/stages/implementation.ts` (Stage 9 per-phase TDD loop), and `src/prompts.ts` (prompt builders), plus the render layer (`src/render/dashboard.ts`, `src/render/stream-theme.ts`) for the Theme parity fix. The codebase has a strong, consistent NEVER-THROW / degrade-to-safe-default discipline (every git/cargo/spawn helper returns `[]` or a conservative verdict on any ambiguity) and a clean scoped-argv + `spawnSync({cwd,timeout,encoding})` envelope — `runRedCheck` and the npm in-scope classifier should reuse `detectProjectCommands`, `resolveTimeoutMs`, `resolveIntegrationStems`, and a shared `touchedFilePaths` extracted from `detectTouchedCargoPackages`. KEY DELTAS to note: (1) Gap 3 is PARTIALLY done — `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE` already forbids `--lib`-only in `buildImplementPrompt` (rust-gated), but `buildTddPrompt` does NOT have it; that's the real gap. (2) The existing Theme regression test (`tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts`) only fakes a `ClassTheme` + calls `initTheme()` for the markdown global — it does NOT obtain the REAL pi Theme proxy object, so `withRealTheme` must first discover the real accessor from `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`'s exports. (3) vitest includes BOTH `tests/**/*.test.ts` AND `src/**/*.test.ts`, and tsconfig `include:["src","tests"]`, so new `tests/helpers/*` and `tests/render/*.test.ts` files are valid without config changes. There is NO long-running API/UI server — the repo is a library; verification is `npm test` (vitest run) + `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit). No new runtime deps permitted.

patterns.name: NEVER-THROW / degrade-to-safe-default (the dominant invariant)
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:485 (detectTouchedCargoPackages wraps git spawns in try/catch → []); :414 (resolveIntegrationStems skips unresolvable); :687 (classifyOutOfScopeErrors conservative on ambiguity); :932 (runBuildGate non-fatal when nothing detected)
patterns.consistency: Pervasive and load-bearing. EVERY new helper (runRedCheck, touchedFilePaths, npm failing-file parser) MUST wrap its whole body in try/catch and return a safe default (unknown / [] / in-scope) on any spawn error, parse ambiguity, or missing runner. This is the single most important convention to mirror; the acceptance criteria re-state it four times.

patterns.name: Per-language project detection via detectProjectCommands
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:852 — returns ProjectCommands with `language` (rust|go|python|frontend|backend|mixed), per-language argv arrays, a `ran` label set, and for npm a `pm` field (detectPm at :842)
patterns.consistency: runRedCheck must branch on `cmds.language` exactly like runBuildGate does (cargo → `-p <pkg> --test <stem>`; npm → `pm run test -- <files>` / direct `vitest run`; pytest → `pytest <files>`). Reuse this rather than re-detecting manifests. Never assume a runner exists — greenfield returns `{language:'mixed',ran:[]}` → red status 'unknown'.

control.title: Technical Specification — Harden super-dev TDD/Implement/Build Cycle (RED oracle, no-`--lib` parity, scope-aware npm gate, render-layer test parity)
control.summary: Pure-TypeScript bug-fix to pi-super-dev closing four proven gaps in the Stage 9 (tdd-guide → implementer → runBuildGate hard oracle) cycle. (Gap 1, CRITICAL) The TDD "red" phase is currently never verified — `src/stages/implementation.ts:70` calls the `tdd-guide` agent and DISCARDS the result, so "TDD" is really "T+D". We add a deterministic, never-throwing `runRedCheck(cwd, testTargets, opts)` oracle (status `red|green|broken|unknown`) modeled on the existing `runBuildGate` skeleton, reusing `detectProjectCommands`, `resolveTimeoutMs`, `resolveIntegrationStems`, and the cached cargo metadata; `implementation.ts` wires a bounded re-prompt loop (re-prompt tdd-guide on `green`/`broken`, ≤ `MAX_RED_RETRIES=2` within the existing attempt; proceed on `red`/`unknown`; loud warning on cap exhaustion). (Gap 3) `buildTddPrompt` already accepts a `langInstructions` arg but `implementation.ts` passes nothing, so the RED phase can miss `tests/` integration binaries — we pass the existing shared `rustDiscipline(setup)` (single `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE` source string) so both RED and implement prompts forbid `--lib`-only and require full `cargo test -p <pkg>` + `--test <stem>` integration targets. (Gap 4) The cargo `inScopePass`/`outOfScopeErrors` concept is cargo-only, so pre-existing npm/vitest/jest failures in untouched files block the gate; we extract a shared `touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef)` git helper (the existing `diff --merge-base` + `ls-files --others` union currently embedded in `detectTouchedCargoPackages` — no new git spawns), parse failing test-file paths from vitest `❯ <path>` / jest `FAIL <path>` markers, and classify in/out-of-scope for npm-family runners exactly like cargo, degrading conservatively to in-scope on any ambiguity. (Gap 2) The fgColors "real runtime shape vs. mock" bug class passed every gate because unit tests used a mock Theme while the real Theme is a class with `this.fgColors`; we deliver `tests/helpers/real-theme.ts` (`withRealTheme<T>(fn:(theme:Theme)=>T):T` exercising the REAL class-based proxy via `initTheme()`, method-style only — never destructured), a `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts` whole-render-layer regression (themeLine, commandBackground, buildResultComponent, packDashboardLines, createDashboardWidgetFactory output), and `docs/testing-parity.md` codifying the convention. Every new gate/oracle/git helper degrades instead of throwing (red→unknown→proceed; parse ambiguity→conservative in-scope; git/spawn error→empty touched set→in-scope); greenfield repos and no-failure repos behave exactly as before; cargo `inScopePass` is byte-for-byte unchanged; `MAX_ATTEMPTS=3`, Stage 10 review, and Stage 11 integration are untouched; no new runtime deps. A fully-mechanical "no framework mocks" graph-based gate is explicitly future work (documented, not delivered). Verification: `npm run typecheck` strict-clean + `npm test` ALL green (existing scoped-args/touched-crates/autoscope/inscope-classification/resolver-validation/nonregression/package-wiring suites updated where signatures change, plus new red-oracle/parity/npm-inscope/implementation-red-loop suites).
control.architecture:
The change is confined to four source modules + the test layer of pi-super-dev. It preserves the dominant NEVER-THROW/degrade-to-safe-default invariant, reuses the existing typed interfaces (`ProjectCommands`, `BuildGateResult`, `GateOptions`), and adds three new typed boundaries. There is no long-running server — verification is `npm test` (vitest run) + `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit).

## Module-by-module technical decisions

### A. `src/build-runner.ts` — three additions + one refactor

1. **Shared git helper (refactor + extraction, Gap 4 foundation).** `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd, baseRef?)` (build-runner.ts:485) currently spawns `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` AND `git -C <cwd> ls-files --others --exclude-standard`, concatenates stdouts, then maps `CRATE_SEGMENT_RE` over the lines and `dedupePreservingOrder`s. EXTRACT the raw path union into a new exported function:
   `export function touchedFilePaths(cwd: string, baseRef?: string): string[]` — returns the dedupePreservingOrder union of both git stdouts as RAW file paths (no crate filtering). Base-ref precedence: `baseRef ?? process.env.SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF ?? "main"`. Whole body in try/catch → `[]`. Then refactor `detectTouchedCargoPackages` to map `CRATE_SEGMENT_RE` over `touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef)` (zero behavior change — keeps the 9 touched-crates/autoscope/nonregression tests green; keeps the "bounded git spawns" invariant: still exactly two git processes). This is the single source of the touched-file set for both cargo and npm in-scope classification.

2. **RED oracle (Gap 1a, AC-01).** Add `export type RedStatus = "red" | "green" | "broken" | "unknown"` and `export interface RedCheckOptions { timeoutMs?: number; signal?: AbortSignal; }` (shares the {timeoutMs?, signal?} shape of `GateOptions`). Implement `export function runRedCheck(cwd: string, testTargets: string[], opts?: RedCheckOptions): RedStatus` modeled on the `runBuildGate` skeleton (build-runner.ts:932):
   - `const cmds = detectProjectCommands(cwd);` (returns `{language, ran[], pm?, ...}`; greenfield/no-manifest → `{language:'mixed', ran:[]}`).
   - If `cmds` has no test runner OR `testTargets.length === 0` → return `"unknown"` (no spawn — greenfield cannot stall the pipeline).
   - Build scoped argv per `cmds.language`, mirroring `runBuildGate`'s branching:
     - `rust` → derive stems via `resolveIntegrationStems(cwd, testTargets)` (build-runner.ts:414; skips unresolvable); for each resolved `(pkg, stem)` run `cargo test -p <pkg> --test <stem>` (NO `--lib`); if no stems resolve, fall back to `cargo test -p <pkg>` for the touched packages.
     - `frontend`/`backend` (npm) → if `cmds.pm`/vitest detected: `vitest run <testTargets>` (or `npm test -- <testTargets>` / `pnpm test -- <testTargets>` via `cmds.pm`).
     - `python` → `pytest <testTargets>`.
   - Execute via the existing envelope: `spawnSync(argv[0], argv.slice(1), { cwd, timeout: resolveTimeoutMs(opts?.timeoutMs), encoding: "utf8" })` (resolveTimeoutMs reads `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TIMEOUT_MS`).
   - Classify the COMBINED stdout+stderr+exit code into exactly one status using per-language heuristics:
     - **cargo**: `"broken"` if output contains `error[E`, `could not compile`, or `no tests to run` with no test execution; `"red"` if exit!==0 AND a failure marker (`test result: FAILED.`, `FAILED`, `panicked`) appears AFTER successful compilation; `"green"` if exit===0 (all passed); `"unknown"` on ambiguous shape.
     - **npm/vitest/jest**: `"broken"` on collection/parse errors (`SyntaxError`, `failed to load`, `No test files found` without a run); `"red"` if exit!==0 with a failing-test marker (`❯`, `FAIL`, `Tests:\s+\d+ failed`); `"green"` if exit===0; `"unknown"` on ambiguity.
     - **pytest**: `"red"` if `failed`/`error` in summary with exit!==0; `"green"` if exit===0; `"broken"` on collection error (`ERROR collecting`); `"unknown"` on ambiguity.
   - **The ENTIRE body is wrapped in try/catch**: any spawn error, timeout, or parse ambiguity → return `"unknown"` (or a conservative status) — NEVER throw. This is the load-bearing invariant mirrored from every existing helper.

3. **npm in-scope classifier (Gap 4, AC-04).** Add `export function parseFailingNpmTestFiles(combinedOutput: string): string[]` matching vitest `❯\s*(<path>)` and jest `^FAIL\s+(<path>)` markers; returns deduped paths or `[]` on no match (never throws). In `runBuildGate`, after a FAILED npm/vitest/jest test step: `const touched = touchedFilePaths(cwd, opts?.gate?.baseRef ?? process.env.SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF);` then `const failing = parseFailingNpmTestFiles(combined);` classify each failing file OUT-of-scope if NOT in `touched`; partition into `outOfScopeErrors` (out-of-scope) vs `errors` (in-scope); set `result.inScopePass = result.pass || (failing.length>0 && failing.every(f => !touched.has(f)))` and populate `result.outOfScopeErrors` — EXACTLY mirroring the cargo path at build-runner.ts:1098-1100 and reusing the existing `BuildGateResult` fields verbatim. On any parse ambiguity, empty `touched`, or unresolvable path → treat as IN-SCOPE (conservative; grants no false green). The cargo branch is untouched byte-for-byte (still uses `detectTouchedCargoPackages` + `classifyOutOfScopeErrors`). Because `implementation.ts:93` already treats `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` as green, NO stage change is required for the npm path.

### B. `src/stages/implementation.ts` — RED enforcement loop (Gap 1b, AC-02) + prompt wiring (Gap 3, AC-03)
- Import `runRedCheck` and `type RedStatus` (alongside existing `runBuildGate, type GateOptions` at implementation.ts:14); add `const MAX_RED_RETRIES = 2;` (module-level, mirrors `MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3`, no new config surface).
- Capture the tdd-guide agent result (currently DISCARDED at implementation.ts:70): `const tdd = await ctx.agent({...tdd-guide...});` and read `const testFiles = (tdd.control as { testFiles?: string[] } | null)?.testFiles ?? [];`.
- Pass `rustDiscipline(setup)` as the `langInstructions` arg to `buildTddPrompt(...)` so the RED-phase prompt carries the no-`--lib` discipline (Gap 3 — buildTddPrompt already accepts the param; the shared `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE` const is the single source of truth). Requires `rustDiscipline` to be exported from prompts.ts if not already.
- Bounded RED loop inside the existing `attempt` loop (does NOT change `MAX_ATTEMPTS=3` or the `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` commit condition at :93):
  ```
  let redStatus: RedStatus = runRedCheck(worktreePath, testFiles, { signal: ctx.signal });
  let retries = 0;
  ctx.log(`Implementation ${phaseId} red-oracle: ${redStatus} (ran: ${testFiles.join(",") || "n/a"})`);
  while ((redStatus === "green" || redStatus === "broken") && retries < MAX_RED_RETRIES) {
    retries++;
    const retry = await ctx.agent({ id: `...tdd.red${retries}...`, agent:"tdd-guide", prompt: buildTddPrompt(..., rustDiscipline(setup)) + redRePromptHint(redStatus) });
    const nextFiles = (retry.control as { testFiles?: string[] } | null)?.testFiles ?? testFiles;
    redStatus = runRedCheck(worktreePath, nextFiles, { signal: ctx.signal });
    ctx.log(`Implementation ${phaseId} red-oracle: ${redStatus} (ran: ${nextFiles.join(",") || "n/a"})`);
  }
  if (redStatus === "green" || redStatus === "broken") ctx.log(`Implementation ${phaseId} red-oracle WARNING: not confirmed-red after ${MAX_RED_RETRIES} retries — proceeding`);
  // proceed to implementer (red OR unknown OR cap-exhausted)
  ```
  The hint helper `redRePromptHint(status)` returns: for `green` — "Your tests passed already; write tests that GENUINELY fail against the unimplemented behavior"; for `broken` — "Your tests did not compile/collect; fix the test so it runs and fails".
- Augment the implementer prompt: when `redStatus === "red"`, append "The TDD tests are CONFIRMED-red; your goal is to make them green." When `redStatus === "unknown"` or cap-exhausted, append a note that red status could not be confirmed.
- NEVER stall: `unknown` proceeds immediately (no re-prompt); cap exhaustion proceeds with a loud warning. Worst-case cost per phase is bounded: `≤2 tdd-guide + ≤2 red-check + 1 implementer + 1 build-gate`.

### C. `src/prompts.ts` — confirm shared source string (AC-03)
- No text change to `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE` (prompts.ts:99) or `rustDiscipline(s)` (prompts.ts:105). The fix is in implementation.ts (pass `rustDiscipline(setup)` as `langInstructions`). Verify both `buildTddPrompt` and `buildImplementPrompt` reference the SAME const (single source of truth). Export `rustDiscipline` if implementation.ts needs it.

### D. Render layer + tests (Gap 2, AC-05)
- `tests/helpers/real-theme.ts`: `withRealTheme<T>(fn: (theme: Theme) => T): T` — calls `initTheme()` (idempotent), obtains the REAL pi Theme proxy from `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` (discover the accessor first — the existing `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` only FAKE-builds a ClassTheme + seeds the markdown global, it never obtains the real proxy), passes it to `fn`. NEVER destructures theme methods (`this.fgColors` crash guard); callers use method-style `theme.fg(...)`.
- `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts`: runs `themeLine`, `commandBackground` (stream-theme.ts:139,206), `buildResultComponent`, `packDashboardLines`, `createDashboardWidgetFactory` output (dashboard.ts:360,208,295) through `withRealTheme`; asserts no-throw + non-empty ANSI output. Generalizes `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` into a whole-render-layer regression.
- `docs/testing-parity.md`: documents that any module wrapping a framework type behind a structural interface MUST have ≥1 parity test via `withRealTheme`/`initTheme`; mock-only coverage of a class-based dependency is a known false-green; a graph-based "no framework mocks" gate is future work.

## File inventory
- **Modify:** `src/build-runner.ts` (add `touchedFilePaths`, `RedStatus`, `RedCheckOptions`, `runRedCheck`, `parseFailingNpmTestFiles`; refactor `detectTouchedCargoPackages`; extend `runBuildGate` npm branch), `src/stages/implementation.ts` (RED loop + import + prompt wiring), `src/prompts.ts` (export `rustDiscipline` if needed), existing `src/build-runner.test.ts` where signatures affect it.
- **Create:** `tests/helpers/real-theme.ts`, `tests/render/real-theme-parity.test.ts`, `docs/testing-parity.md`, plus new suites `tests/red-oracle.test.ts`, `tests/npm-inscope.test.ts`, `tests/implementation-red-loop.test.ts` (or colocated in existing test files per repo convention).
- **Delete:** none. No config edits needed (vitest globs `tests/**` + `src/**`; tsconfig `include:["src","tests"]`).

## Cross-cutting constraints honored
- NEVER destructure pi Theme methods (class with `this.fgColors`); keep `tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts` green.
- NEVER throw from any new gate/oracle/git helper.
- Backward compatible: greenfield → `unknown` → proceed; cargo `inScopePass` unchanged; `MAX_ATTEMPTS=3`, Stage 10/11 structure, `nodes.ts`/`workflow.ts`/`pipeline.ts` untouched; no new runtime deps; no new git spawns beyond the existing diff+untracked union (shared helper).
control.testingStrategy:
Verification is `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit, strict, no `any` leaks) + `npm test` (vitest run). The repo is a library — no API/UI server. Every phase ships its own independently-runnable test suite; the final phase is the full-suite + regression gate. Test conventions mirror the existing suite: `vi.mock('node:child_process', () => ({ spawnSync:(cmd,argv)=>{...} }))` with a module-level mock-state object reset per test (src/build-runner.test.ts:39-40).

**Per-phase testable units (each phase independently green):**
- **Phase 1 (touchedFilePaths):** new unit tests — committed-diff paths, untracked-only paths, both-union dedup ordering, spawn error → `[]`, empty repo → `[]`, base-ref precedence. Regression: existing touched-crates/autoscope/nonregression suites stay green (zero behavior change from the refactor).
- **Phase 2 (runRedCheck):** new `tests/red-oracle.test.ts` — `vi.mock` spawnSync stub returning fixed stdout/exit per case → assert `red` (cargo `test result: FAILED.` + exit≠0; vitest `❯` failing + exit≠0), `green` (exit 0), `broken` (cargo `error[E`/`could not compile`; vitest `SyntaxError`/collection error), `unknown` (no manifest / empty testTargets / spawn error / parse ambiguity). Assert NEVER throws (wrap a throwing spawnSync → expect `unknown`).
- **Phase 3 (RED loop):** `tests/implementation-red-loop.test.ts` — `vi.mock` runRedCheck returning green→green→red (assert ≤`MAX_RED_RETRIES` re-prompts), green-after-cap (assert loud warning + proceed), unknown (assert immediate proceed, zero re-prompts), red (assert zero re-prompts + implementer prompt contains "CONFIRMED-red"). Assert `MAX_ATTEMPTS=3` outer loop and `gate.pass||gate.inScopePass` commit condition unchanged.
- **Phase 4 (no-`--lib` parity):** prompt snapshot test — assert the tdd prompt for a `rust` setup contains the no-`--lib` clause + `cargo test --test <stem>` instruction, and a non-rust setup omits it; assert buildTddPrompt and buildImplementPrompt share the identical `RUST_SELF_VERIFY_DISCIPLINE` substring.
- **Phase 5 (npm in-scope):** `tests/npm-inscope.test.ts` — stubbed failing vitest stdout (`❯ src/untouched.test.ts`) + stubbed git diff → assert `outOfScopeErrors` populated + `inScopePass=true`; a touched-file failure → `inScopePass=false` (blocks); unparseable output → conservative in-scope; `touchedFilePaths` spawn error → in-scope; cargo branch unchanged (existing inscope-classification suite still green byte-for-byte).

result.text:
I have enough grounding to render a verdict. Key verified facts:
- `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (build-runner.ts:485), `runBuildGate` (:932), `resolveTimeoutMs` (:84), `resolveIntegrationStems` (:414 returning `string[]` stems only), `detectProjectCommands` (:852, `{language,ran,pm?...}`, greenfield→`{language:"mixed",ran:[]}`), `inScopePass` formula (:1099) — all confirmed.
- Render fns at stream-theme.ts:139/206 and dashboard.ts:208/295/360 — all confirmed.
- **Gap 3 premise is factually wrong**: implementation.ts:70-71 already passes `lang` (specialist.languageInstructions) as `langInstructions`, not "nothing". Literal fix would clobber specialist output.
- `resolveIntegrationStems` returns stems only — no `(pkg,stem)` tuple source for runRedCheck's `cargo test -p <pkg> --test <stem>`.
- `initTheme()` real-accessor parity already exists in 3 render tests; no `Theme` type export found from the pi package.

control.title: Spec Review — Harden super-dev TDD/Implement/Build Cycle (RED oracle, no-`--lib` parity, scope-aware npm gate, render-layer test parity)
control.summary: Pure-TS bug-fix spec for four gaps in the Stage 9 (tdd-guide→implementer→runBuildGate) cycle. The module/function/line grounding is strong overall (~85%): detectTouchedCargoPackages, runBuildGate, resolveTimeoutMs, resolveIntegrationStems, detectProjectCommands, the cargo inScopePass formula, and all five render-layer functions are exactly where cited, with correct shapes. The degrade-instead-of-throw invariant and MAX_ATTEMPTS=3/Stage 10-11-untouched constraints are honored. However two content defects will cause implementation failure if taken literally: (1) the central premise of Gap 3 is factually false — implementation.ts:70-71 ALREADY passes the route-specialist's languageInstructions as buildTddPrompt's langInstructions arg, so the literal prescribed fix (pass rustDiscipline(setup) as that arg) would CLOBBER specialist instructions, a silent regression; and (2) the runRedCheck cargo branch is ungrounded — resolveIntegrationStems returns stem strings only (no pkg), and runBuildGate runs stems as `cargo test --test <stem>` WITHOUT `-p`, contradicting the spec's `cargo test -p <pkg> --test <stem>` with no defined source for <pkg>. Secondary issues: the `Theme` type the parity helper depends on is not exported from the pi package and its accessor is deferred ("discover first"); the broken/red status boundary heuristics are not crisply measurable; and Gap 2's novelty is overstated since initTheme()-based real-accessor parity testing already exists in three render tests. Verdict REVISIONS NEEDED: the false Gap 3 premise and ungrounded cargo oracle are exactly the class of content defects that produce wrong implementations.

findings.title: Gap 3 premise is factually false — implementation.ts already passes langInstructions; literal fix clobbers specialist output
findings.detail: Spec §B/C state 'buildTddPrompt already accepts a langInstructions arg but implementation.ts passes nothing' and prescribe 'Pass rustDiscipline(setup) as the langInstructions arg to buildTddPrompt'. Verified against src/stages/implementation.ts:70-71: `const lang = (specialist.value.languageInstructions as string) ?? "";` then `buildTddPrompt(setup, state.classify ?? null, phase, state.spec ?? null, lang)`. A non-empty value IS passed today (the route-specialist language instructions). Replacing it with `rustDiscipline(setup)` discards the specialist's per-task language instructions — a silent behavior regression for every non-Rust stack and for Rust tasks carrying specialist guidance. Fix direction: CONCATENATE (`lang + (lang?"\n":"") + rustDiscipline(setup)`) or append, not replace. The spec's premise and its one-line fix are both wrong.

findings.title: runRedCheck cargo branch ungrounded — resolveIntegrationStems returns stems only; no (pkg,stem) tuple source; contradicts runBuildGate
findings.detail: Spec §A.2 cargo branch: 'derive stems via resolveIntegrationStems(cwd, testTargets) ... for each resolved (pkg, stem) run cargo test -p <pkg> --test <stem> ... fall back to cargo test -p <pkg> for the touched packages'. Verified src/build-runner.ts:414 resolveIntegrationStems returns `string[]` (stems only, derived from file paths, never a package name) — there is no pkg in its return, so the (pkg,stem) iteration has no defined <pkg> source. Moreover runBuildGate itself runs integration stems as `cargo test --test <stem>` WITHOUT `-p` (per the CR-004/CR-008 comment), so the RED oracle's cargo invocation diverges from and contradicts the very build gate it is modeled on. The 'touched packages' fallback references detectTouchedCargoPackages output that runRedCheck never obtains. Recommend runRedCheck cargo branch mirror runBuildGate exactly (stems via `cargo test --test <stem>`, packages via the same detectTouchedCargoPackages→resolveCargoPackageNames tier) so the two oracles agree.

findings.title: withRealTheme helper signature references a Theme type that is not exported by the pi package
findings.detail: Spec §D: withRealTheme<T>(fn:(theme:Theme)=>T):T 'exercising the REAL class-based proxy'. grep of @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent type declarations found no exported `Theme` type; the three existing render parity tests (dashboard-result.test.ts:35, dashboard-result-perkind.test.ts:37, regression-guard.test.ts:38) import only `initTheme` and exercise the `getMarkdownTheme()` MODULE GLOBAL, not a returned Theme object. The spec itself defers the accessor ('discover the accessor first'). The helper's `(theme:Theme)` parameter type is therefore ungrounded until that discovery is done; the signature should be left as discovered-accessor-typed or the spec must name the concrete accessor/type to import.

findings.title: Gap 2 novelty overstated — real-accessor (initTheme) parity testing already exists in three render tests
findings.detail: Spec §D frames the gap as 'the existing test only fakes ClassTheme ... never obtains the real proxy'. Verified initTheme() real-accessor parity is already an established pattern in src/render/dashboard-result.test.ts, dashboard-result-perkind.test.ts, and regression-guard.test.ts (beforeAll(() => initTheme())). The spec's premise that no real-Theme parity exists is inaccurate; the genuine residual gap is narrower (a whole-render-layer SWEEP + a documented convention), not the introduction of real-accessor testing itself. Re-scope the justification to avoid implementing a duplicate of existing coverage.

findings.title: broken/red status boundary heuristics are not crisply measurable
findings.detail: Spec §A.2 classification rules are stated in natural language without an operational test, e.g. cargo 'red if exit!==0 AND a failure marker ... appears AFTER successful compilation' and 'broken if ... no tests to run with no test execution'. There is no rule for detecting 'after successful compilation' or 'no test execution' from the COMBINED stdout+stderr blob — these require a state marker the spec does not define. Per-status stubbed tests (Phase 2) therefore cannot be authored deterministically against the stated boundary; implementers will invent their own marker logic. Specify the concrete precedence/regex sequence (e.g. 'broken if error[E|could not compile present; else red if exit≠0 & /test result: FAILED\.|FAILED|panicked/ present; else green if exit=0; else unknown') so the red/broken tests are reproducible.

findings.title: ACs and SCENARIOs are referenced inline but not enumerated or cross-mapped in this spec
findings.detail: Spec cites AC-01..AC-05 inline (no 'Acceptance Criteria' section listing them) and lists SCENARIO-001..022 by ID only under 'BDD Scenario References' with no SCENARIO→AC→phase→task matrix in-document. The artifacts exist in the spec directory (01-requirements.md, 02-bdd-scenarios.md, 07-implementation-plan.md, 08-task-list.md), but the task header marked Plan/Tasks as N/A despite their presence, and this document does not embed or link the traceability. D5 traceability cannot be confirmed from the spec alone; at minimum an AC list with a SCENARIO→AC map should be present so the reviewer/implementation phase can verify every AC has a satisfying spec section and every SCENARIO has a phase.

findings.title: RED-loop cost bound is stated per-attempt, not per-phase; redRePromptHint helper missing from file inventory
findings.detail: Spec §B claims worst-case '≤2 tdd-guide + ≤2 red-check + 1 implementer + 1 build-gate' but this is PER ATTEMPT inside MAX_ATTEMPTS=3; the true per-phase worst case is ~3× (up to 6 tdd-guide + 6 red-check + 3 implementer + 3 build-gate). The per-attempt framing could mislead sizing/review. Separately, the loop pseudocode calls redRePromptHint(status) but that helper is not listed in the 'File inventory → Create/Modify' list; add it to the inventory so it is not missed.

findings.title: runRedCheck reconstructs npm/pytest argv instead of reusing cmds.test — divergence from runBuildGate is unstated
findings.detail: runBuildGate executes tests via the existing `cmds.test` array (e.g. ['pytest','-q'], npm/pm scripts) detected by detectProjectCommands. Spec §A.2 instead has runRedCheck reconstruct argv from cmds.pm / vitest detection / explicit pytest <testTargets>, diverging from how the hard oracle invokes the same toolchain. The rationale (scoped per-file testTargets) is plausible but unstated; either reuse cmds.test where testTargets is empty, or document why the two gates intentionally invoke differently, to avoid drift between the RED oracle and the build gate.

dimensions.name: D1 Completeness
dimensions.notes: Error/degrade paths (never-throw, red→unknown→proceed, empty-touched→in-scope) are thoroughly specified. Gap: AC-01..05 and SCENARIO-001..022 are referenced but not enumerated or mapped in-document; traceability lives only in sibling artifacts. Score 3/5.

dimensions.name: D2 Consistency
dimensions.notes: Two substantive inconsistencies: Gap 3 premise ('implementation.ts passes nothing') contradicts code (it passes specialist langInstructions); runRedCheck cargo invocation (`cargo test -p <pkg> --test <stem>`) contradicts runBuildGate (`cargo test --test <stem>`). NFRs (typecheck strict, no new runtime deps) consistent. Score 3/5.

dimensions.name: D3 Feasibility
dimensions.notes: Core skeleton is feasible and reuses real helpers. Feasibility gap: runRedCheck cargo <pkg> has no defined source given resolveIntegrationStems returns stems only; Theme accessor for withRealTheme is deferred ('discover first'). Score 3/5.

dimensions.name: D4 Testability
dimensions.notes: Per-phase stubbed-stdout test suites are well enumerated; degrade/never-throw assertions are concrete. Gap: the cargo/npm broken-vs-red boundary rules are not crisply measurable (no operational marker for 'after successful compilation'), making deterministic red/broken test cases hard to author. Score 3/5.

dimensions.name: D5 Traceability
dimensions.notes: Module→gap→AC→phase narrative is traceable in prose, but no AC list or SCENARIO→AC→phase matrix is embedded; 22 scenarios appear only as IDs. Sibling artifacts exist but are not linked/summarized here. Score 3/5.

dimensions.name: D6 Grounding
dimensions.notes: ~85% verified: detectTouchedCargoPackages(:485), runBuildGate(:932), resolveTimeoutMs(:84), resolveIntegrationStems(:414), detectProjectCommands(:852, mixed/greenfield shape), inScopePass(:1099), and all five render fns (stream-theme.ts:139/206; dashboard.ts:208/295/360) are accurate. Two HIGH grounding defects: false Gap 3 premise and ungrounded cargo (pkg,stem). Below the 90% bar. Score 3/5.

dimensions.name: D7 Complexity
dimensions.notes: Proportional file inventory; degrades instead of throwing; shared git helper avoids new spawns; cargo branch byte-for-byte unchanged. Minor: per-phase cost bound understated (per-attempt framing) and redRePromptHint omitted from inventory. Score 4/5.

dimensions.name: D8 Ambiguity
dimensions.notes: Degrade/precedence rules (base-ref ?? env ?? main; never-throw) are explicit. Ambiguity: Theme type/accessor undefined; broken/red heuristics imprecise; runRedCheck argv reconstruction diverges from cmds.test without rationale. Score 3/5.

result.text: All 30 tests fail with `touchedFilePaths is not a function` — confirmed RED state. The function doesn't exist yet, exactly as intended for TDD. The tests cover all Phase 1 acceptance criteria: raw path mapping (no crate filtering), union dedup ordering, safe degradation to `[]`, never-throws invariant, base-ref precedence, and the two-spawn discrete-argv shape.

control.summary:
Wrote the RED-phase test suite for Phase P1 (extract shared `touchedFilePaths(cwd, baseRef?): string[]` git helper from `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, src/build-runner.ts:485). All 30 tests fail with `touchedFilePaths is not a function` — confirmed RED state, as the export does not exist yet.

The new file `tests/build-runner-touched-file-paths.test.ts` (hermetic: `vi.mock("node:child_process")`, no real git) covers every P1 acceptance criterion and edge case from the specification, ordered simplest→boundary→error:

1. Raw path mapping (5): returns file paths VERBATIM with NO crate-segment filtering — the critical P1 distinction from `detectTouchedCargoPackages`. Includes committed-diff paths, untracked-only paths, and non-crate paths (Cargo.toml/README/docs) that the Phase 5 npm classifier needs.
2. Union + dedup ordering (4): committed-diff-first union with untracked, dedup of a path in both listings to one entry at first-seen position, interleaved-order preservation, untracked-only-with-empty-diff.
3. Safe degradation to [] (6): non-zero git exit, both-spawn ENOENT, one-fail-one-empty, empty both, whitespace-only, the empty-set-is-conservative-fallback contract.
4. Never throws (3): spawnSync throws → [], non-string stdout → [], only-untracked-spawn-throws → [].
5. Base-ref precedence (5): default `main`, `SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF` env, explicit arg wins over env, `-C <cwd>`, and the untracked spawn must NOT carry `--merge-base`.
